title: SPADEX Mission and Orbital Docking
focus: ISRO's indigenous satellite docking technology and its strategic significance for future space stations.
rationale: A distinct ISRO mission focused on orbital docking, which is a crucial standalone concept for the upcoming Bharatiya Antariksh Station.
sectionExcerpt: ISRO achieved second successful satellite docking under SpaDeX Mission. The docking technology (joining of two fast-moving satellites in space), indigenously developed by ISRO, is known as the Bhartiya Docking System. Objective: To develop and demonstrate the technology needed for rendezvous, docking, and undocking of two small spacecraft (SDX01 (Chaser) and SDX02 (Target)) in a low-Earth circular orbit. The mission made India the fourth country in the world to achieve satellite docking in space, joining the ranks of the US, Russia, and China.
evidenceBullets: SpaDeX Mission involves the rendezvous and docking of two small spacecraft: SDX01 (Chaser) and SDX02 (Target) in a low-Earth circular orbit.
The indigenous docking technology developed by ISRO is called the Bhartiya Docking System.
India is the fourth country to achieve satellite docking in space, following the US, Russia, and China.
The SpaDeX spacecraft were designed by the UR Rao Satellite Centre (URSC).

title: Chandrayaan-3 and Lunar Exploration
focus: Chandrayaan-3's achievements, payloads, and related lunar missions like NASA's GRAIL and lunar far-side geography.
rationale: Groups India's lunar mission with NASA's lunar gravity mission and geographical facts about the Moon's far side, forming a cohesive lunar exploration cluster.
sectionExcerpt: Chandra's Surface Thermophysical Experiment (ChaSTE) of Chandrayaan-3 became the first mission to successfully penetrate the soil of a celestial body to deploy a thermal probe. Became the world's first mission to soft-land near the lunar South Pole. The landing site was named as 'Shiv Shakti' point. NASA's GRAIL Mission created the most detailed map of the Moon's gravity. The Moon's nearside (facing Earth) is warmer and more geologically active than the farside. About the far side of the Moon: Refers to the hemisphere of the Moon that always faces away from Earth because the Moon is tidally locked with Earth. It has a thicker crust, more craters, and fewer lava plains than near side.
evidenceBullets: Chandrayaan-3's ChaSTE payload successfully penetrated lunar soil to deploy a thermal probe.
The Chandrayaan-3 landing site near the lunar South Pole is named 'Shiv Shakti' point.
Japan became the fifth country to soft-land on the Moon with its SLIM mission.
NASA's GRAIL Mission mapped the Moon's gravity, revealing the nearside is warmer and more geologically active.
The Moon's far side is tidally locked, has a thicker crust, more craters, and fewer lava plains than the near side.

title: Axiom-4 Mission and the International Space Station
focus: India's participation in the private Axiom-4 mission to the ISS and ISRO's microgravity research experiments.
rationale: Covers human spaceflight, the specific Axiom-4 mission details, ISRO's biological experiments in space, and ISS facts.
sectionExcerpt: The Axiom-4 Mission carrying Indian astronaut Group Captain Shubhanshu Shukla and 3 other astronauts successfully returned. Ax-4 is a 4th private astronaut mission, to the International Space Station (ISS), of private US Company Axiom Space in collaboration with NASA and SpaceX. Shubhanshu Shukla is the first Indian astronaut to board the ISS and only the second Indian to travel to space after Rakesh Sharma in 1984. Key Research and activities executed by ISRO: Crop growth, Cyanobacteria, Space Microalgae, Myogenesis, Tardigrades. About International Space Station (ISS): A habitable artificial satellite in Low Earth orbit (LEO). Key partners for ISS: Europe (ESA), the USA (NASA), Japan (JAXA), Canada (CSA) and Russia (Roscosmos).
evidenceBullets: Axiom-4 is a private astronaut mission to the ISS by Axiom Space, NASA, and SpaceX, launched via the Falcon 9 vehicle.
Group Captain Shubhanshu Shukla is the first Indian astronaut to board the ISS and the second Indian in space.
ISRO executed microgravity research on Ax-4 involving crop growth, cyanobacteria, space microalgae, myogenesis, and tardigrades (water bears).
The ISS is a habitable artificial satellite in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) partnered by ESA, NASA, JAXA, CSA, and Roscosmos.
